title: Hold-out beta or Venn-Abers calibration on blended/rank-based scores
summary: ROC AUC is ranking-based, but post-hoc monotone calibration can still improve blend behavior, threshold stability, and public-LB robustness when combining heterogeneous specialists.
rationale: ROC AUC is ranking-based, but post-hoc monotone calibration can still improve blend behavior, threshold stability, and public-LB robustness when combining heterogeneous specialists. Recent calibration literature and classic beta-calibration results suggest isotonic is not always best, especially on moderate calibration sets.
implementation_hint: Reserve a race-grouped calibration fold after model training. Compare raw scores, isotonic, beta calibration, and Venn-Abers on a single model and on any ensemble or ranker-derived score. Use calibration only as a final mapping layer and validate on untouched grouped folds to ensure AUC does not degrade materially.
expected_effect: Likely a small but cheap improvement in ensemble quality and probability realism; especially useful if trying separate specialists whose score scales differ.
risk: Calibration cannot create signal and may slightly hurt AUC if the calibration fold is too small or distribution-shifted; needs strict grouped holdout.
